attributes.description: Alerts when an EBS volume attached to an EC2 instance fails its status check, indicating the volume is unreachable or unable to complete I/O operations. Distinct from the instance and system status checks.
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] EBS attached volume status check failed

investigation_guide.blob:
## EC2 EBS Attached Volume Status Check Failed

### Meaning
The **EBS attached status check** monitors whether EBS volumes attached to an EC2 instance are reachable and able to complete I/O operations. A failure indicates the volume has become unreachable — distinct from the OS-level instance check (`StatusCheckFailed_Instance`) and the AWS host-level system check (`StatusCheckFailed_System`).

### Remediation
1. Check the [AWS Health Dashboard](https://health.aws.amazon.com/) for ongoing EBS or availability zone events.
2. **Stop and start** the instance — this moves it to new host hardware and can restore EBS connectivity. A reboot alone is insufficient.
3. If the volume remains impaired, create a snapshot and restore from it to a new volume.
4. Open an AWS support case if the issue persists after stop/start.

### Common causes
- Hardware failure on the storage subsystem underlying the EBS volume
- Physical host connectivity issues between the instance and the EBS volume
- Underlying infrastructure impairment in the availability zone

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND attributes.MetricName == "StatusCheckFailed_AttachedEBS"
    AND attributes.stat == "Maximum"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(InstanceId, " (", resource.attributes.cloud.region, ")")
| STATS max_failed = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed_AttachedEBS`) BY ec2_instance
// 1 = failed — Maximum stat catches any failure within the period
| WHERE max_failed >= 1
| SORT max_failed DESC
| LIMIT 100
